candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A summary line, but it is not identified as COGS/cost-of-sales related specifically. Adding it to COGS would risk misallocation/double counting versus SG&A or mixed operating buckets.
Separate operating-level depreciation component, but not labelled as belonging to COGS specifically.
Explicitly R&D-related, so not a COGS D&A row.
Separate amortization line, but not labelled as COGS/cost-of-sales related.
Balancing line, not a clean operating D&A bucket to add to COGS.

variables.notes: No explicit D&A row is identified as belonging to Materials, goods and services / COGS. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines but unallocated across operating functions, so conservatively do not add any to COGS_DA.

candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A summary line, but not clearly attributable to the selected SG&A component bucket versus COGS or mixed operating functions.
Separate operating-level depreciation component, but not labelled as SG&A specifically.

Explicit R&D-related amortization above operating profit. Since the only selected XRD row is a supplemental/below-EBIT R&D row and there is no separate above-EBIT R&D expense row included symmetrically in both XSGA and XRD, this is the clearest operating D&A row that should be added separately within the broader XSGA/overhead bucket.
Separate operating amortization line, but not clearly SG&A-specific and could relate to multiple functions.
Balancing line, not a clean D&A expense row to add.

variables.notes: Selected only the explicit R&D D&A line. Generic D&A rows are separate from operating expenses but are not clearly assignable to SG&A versus COGS, so they are excluded to avoid misallocation/double counting. The R&D amortization line is a special-case operating D&A item that can be added to XSGA_DA because the mapped XRD row comes from a supplemental/below-EBIT section rather than an above-EBIT R&D expense row.
